title: China Confirms Urumqi Talks 'Advancing'; Shelling Continues Simultaneously
detail: China's government confirmed that Pakistan-Afghanistan peace talks in Urumqi were 'advancing', with both sides engaging in substantive discussions. However, Pakistani artillery and drone strikes on Kunar, Paktika, and Khost provinces continued simultaneously with the negotiations, underscoring the fragility of the diplomatic process. Pakistan fired an additional 185 long-range artillery shells into a single district in Kunar on April 3, wounding 10 people. Analysts noted that Pakistan appeared to be using military pressure as a negotiating lever during the talks, continuing operations while maintaining a diplomatic channel to demonstrate leverage and assert conditions for any ceasefire agreement.

title: Afghan Economy Minister: 27,000+ Families Displaced by Pakistan Operations Across Nine Provinces
detail: Afghanistan's Economy Minister Qari Din Mohammad Hanif reported that more than 27,000 Afghan families across nine provinces had been displaced by Pakistan's military operations under Operation Ghazab-il-Haq since the conflict intensified on February 26. Many of those displaced lacked access to food, shelter, and healthcare. The figure represented a significant increase from the 120,000+ individuals reported by UNHCR earlier in March. Displacement was concentrated in Kunar, Nangarhar, Khost, Paktika, Paktia, Kandahar, and surrounding border provinces. The minister called on the international community to provide emergency humanitarian assistance to displaced families and urged Pakistan to halt strikes on civilian areas.

title: PICSS: Pakistan Records 35% Drop in Terrorist Fatalities in March as Op Ghazab Delivers Security Gains
detail: The Pakistan Institute for Conflict and Security Studies (PICSS) published data showing a 35% decline in terrorism-related fatalities in Pakistan in March 2026 compared to February, coinciding with the launch of Operation Ghazab-il-Haq. Overall combat and terrorism deaths fell from 506 (February) to 331 (March). Terror incidents declined 59% nationally, with Khyber Pakhtunkhwa specifically seeing a 65% drop from 240 incidents in February to 80 in March. Civilian fatalities fell approximately 70%, from 132 to 39. Pakistan's government cited these statistics as evidence that the cross-border military campaign was achieving its stated objective of suppressing TTP attacks on Pakistani territory, pushing back on international criticism of the operations.
